2604.11334.title: Dynamic Summary Generation for Interpretable Multimodal Depression Detection
2604.11334.paper_summary: Depression remains widely underdiagnosed and undertreated because stigma and subjective symptom ratings hinder reliable screening. To address this challenge, we propose a coarse-to-fine, multi-stage framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) for accurate and interpretable detection. The pipeline performs binary screening, five-class severity classification, and continuous regression. At each stage, an LLM produces progressively richer clinical summaries that guide a multimodal fusion module integrating text, audio, and video features, yielding predictions with transparent rationale. The system then consolidates all summaries into a concise, human-readable assessment report. Experiments on the E-DAIC and CMDC datasets show significant improvements over state-of-the-art baselines in both accuracy and interpretability.
2604.11334.paper_summary_zh: 憂鬱症因為污名化和主觀症狀評估的限制而普遍被低估和治療不足。為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了一個粗到細的多階段框架，利用大型語言模型（LLMs）進行準確且可解釋的檢測。該流程執行二元篩查、五類嚴重程度分類和連續回歸。在每個階段，LLM 生成逐步豐富的臨床摘要，指導一個多模態融合模塊，整合文本、音頻和視頻特徵，從而產生具有透明理由的預測。然後，系統將所有摘要整合成一份簡明易懂的評估報告。在 E-DAIC 和 CMDC 數據集上的實驗顯示，在準確性和可解釋性方面，相較於最先進的基準有顯著改善。
2604.11334.author: Shiyu Teng et.al.
2604.11334.authors: Shiyu Teng, Jiaqing Liu, Hao Sun, Yu Li, Shurong Chai, Ruibo Hou, Tomoko Tateyama, Lanfen Lin, Yen-Wei Chen
